name: Oregon HB 2021
source: Oregon House Bill 2021, 81st Legislative Assembly, 2021 Regular Session

promises.body: Reduce greenhouse gas emissions 80% below baseline by 2030, 90% by 2035, 100% by 2040
promises.note: PGE has made progress but is behind the 2030 interim target. Current trajectory suggests 60-65% reduction by 2030.

promises.body: File clean energy plan with PUC demonstrating pathway to 100% clean electricity

promises.body: Reduce greenhouse gas emissions 80% below baseline by 2030, 90% by 2035, 100% by 2040
promises.note: PacifiCorp's multi-state operations and heavy coal dependence make the 2030 target effectively unreachable. Current trajectory: 35-40% by 2030.

promises.body: Establish and enforce emissions reduction targets for investor-owned utilities

promises.body: Allocate minimum 50% of energy program spending to environmental justice communities
promises.note: PGE has allocated approximately 35% of relevant program spending to EJ communities. Tracking mechanisms are in place.

promises.body: Provide direct benefits to environmental justice communities from clean energy transition
promises.note: No quantitative metric or independent verification mechanism exists for 'direct benefits.' The promise is structurally unverifiable.

commitment.hash: placeholder — no source document exists; promise is structurally unverifiable
commitment.sourceDigest: No verification mechanism — 'direct benefits' undefined in statute

promises.body: Ensure energy burden for low-income customers does not increase due to clean energy transition
promises.note: Energy burden is tracked at aggregate level but not at the household level required to verify this promise. No causal isolation between transition costs and other rate factors.

commitment.hash: placeholder — no source document exists; promise is structurally unverifiable

promises.body: Conduct regular rate impact assessments to protect consumers from excessive transition costs
promises.note: PUC conducts regular rate impact analyses as part of IRP and CEP review processes.

promises.body: Cap annual rate increases attributable to clean energy transition at 6% for PGE, 6% for PacifiCorp
promises.note: Rate increases are approaching but have not yet exceeded the cap. PGE's 2024 rate case attributed ~4.5% to clean energy costs. Future years may trigger the cap, which could pause transition investments.

verification.metric: % rate increase attributable to clean energy

promises.body: Publish biennial report on clean energy transition progress, including emissions, costs, and equity impacts
promises.note: DOE published its first biennial report in September 2023. Comprehensive data on emissions trajectories and cost impacts.

promises.body: Conduct meaningful consultation with Oregon's nine federally recognized tribes on clean energy planning
promises.note: PUC has initiated tribal consultation but feedback indicates it has been procedural rather than substantive. Tribes report limited influence on final planning decisions.
